title: Evaluating and improving LLM-based competitive program generation
summary: This study evaluates and improves the generation of competitive programming solutions by large language models (LLMs). The authors construct a benchmark of 80 problems from 2024 ICPC and CCPC regional contests to mitigate data leakage and ensure diverse difficulty levels. Using DeepSeek-R1 with a basic prompt, only 5 out of 80 problems are fully accepted. A fine-grained error taxonomy is built to categorize failures, revealing common design and boundary errors. A targeted improvement framework combining multi-turn dialog repair and information-augmented regeneration raises the number of correct solutions to 46 out of 80, demonstrating significant gains.
author: Minnan Wei, Ziming Li, Xiang Chen, Menglin Zheng, Ziyan Qu, Cheng Yu, Siyu Chen, Xiaolin Ju
